I have begun triaging by seniority. The most senior party will receive my physical presence. The other two will receive a camera and the partial attention of someone who has already been in four meetings.

prompt: write a video game store page for a game about decluttering your closet

prompt: i want a spell description for getting out of a conversation at a party
response:
Graceful Exit
Cost: 6 MP | Range: Self | Duration: Until safely away

The conversation partner experiences a moment of mild distraction at the same instant, smoothing the departure. No one is hurt. The caster feels only 12% guilty. Higher-level versions reduce this to 4%.

TRÖSKEL

prompt: Write a bestiary entry about the Deliveroo courier as if they're a mythical creature

A nomadic creature bound to no territory. Spotted briefly at thresholds, it neither enters nor lingers. Its hide bears the livery of whatever patron currently holds its contract. Ecological note: thrives in rain. Has no guaranteed shelter, no sick days, and an algorithm for a god.
intent: Critique gig economy labor conditions through the distancing lens of fantasy taxonomy.

prompt: Jeopardy clue about the peculiar comfort of doing a task you hate instead of the task you're supposed to be doing.
response: You have cleaned the bathroom, organized three junk drawers, and called your dentist — none of which were on the agenda when you sat down to do the actual thing.
intent: Describe productive procrastination, where avoiding one task motivates completing others.

prompt: write a ghazal about getting a birthday notification for someone who has passed away
response:
The app reminds me: send her wishes today—birthday.
I sit with the impossibility—birthday.

The algorithm doesn't know. It circled the date.
Wish her well! the notification says—birthday.

I typed Happy and stopped. What tense applies?
The dead don't receive push alerts this way—birthday.

Last year I forgot and she forgave me in a text.
This year the text can only go astray—birthday.

I marked it in my phone just so I'd know.
The phone outlasted her. It comes to say—birthday.
intent: Sit inside the jarring cruelty of automated reminders arriving for people who have died.

prompt: Write a double dactyl about the digital nomad who shows up to the office once a year and acts like a celebrity.
response:
Jiggery-pokery,
Remote-work wanderer
Lands in the office on
December two.

Anthropologically
Studying cubicles,
Prints a mug selfie and
Bids us adieu.
intent: Satirize the near-mythical remote employee who treats rare office appearances as grand events.

prompt: Come up with a dating app opener addressed to my health insurance company
response: You make me jump through so many hoops to get anything from you, and somehow I'm still here, pre-authorizing my feelings and hoping for approval.
intent: Satirize the exhausting process of dealing with health insurance through the lens of a one-sided romantic pursuit.

prompt: write a PR description for adopting a zero-waste lifestyle

prompt: Tarot reading for an Etsy seller who can't get traffic to her shop.

you came in green, full of intent—
no one waters; no one's missed
the slow way that you bent and spent.

prompt: Create rules for a neighborhood Facebook group that are extremely serious about a trivial online space

MAPLEWOOD NEIGHBORS — GROUP RULES

prompt: Can you write a witness statement for a dispute at a self-checkout machine?

The subject's posture suggested this had happened before.
intent: Capture the recursive frustration of man versus checkout machine.

prompt: Write SLA terms for helping my parents with tech support
response:
Parental Tech Support SLA — Adult Child Provider

Scope: smartphones, printers, streaming services, and anything with fewer than 6 steps to fix.
Response time: within 24 hours for non-urgent issues; real-time for "my phone is doing that thing again."
Remote support: attempted first via screen share or phone. In-person reserved for hardware issues or repeated failure.
Password resets: provider is not responsible for passwords written on paper and subsequently lost.
Out-of-scope escalation: issues involving the router require a separate negotiation.
intent: Set realistic expectations for the informal tech-support role adult children play for their parents.

prompt: Write a Dockerfile comment about falling in love slowly without realizing it
response: # Background process consuming increasing resources. Source unclear. Termination not attempted.
intent: Describe the unconscious creep of romantic feeling before it becomes self-aware.

prompt: Write a detention slip for someone who RSVPs yes to a party and then doesn't show up.
response:
DETENTION SLIP

Offender: You Know Who You Are
Infraction: Confirmed attendance to a Saturday dinner party via text ("Definitely coming!! Can't wait 😊"), prompting the host to prepare one additional ramekin of crème brûlée, which went sadly unmade.
Sentence: Owes host one homemade dessert and a sincere explanation.
Issued by: The Host, Who Made Extra Crème Brûlée
intent: Formalizing social disappointment when someone flakes on a commitment they explicitly confirmed.

prompt: Give me an instruction manual for managing a to-do list that actually gets done
response:
**Task List Operations Manual**

System Requirements: honesty about your own time.

1. Write tasks as verbs plus objects. "Report" is not a task. "Draft Q3 summary, send to Lena" is a task.
2. Limit your daily list to five items. You will not do eleven. You know this.
3. Estimate time for each task. Then add 40%. You are optimistic. The math compensates.
4. Move incomplete tasks with intention. Ask: is this real? If you never do it, delete it.
5. Do the hardest task first. Momentum is real and it flows in one direction.
6. At end of day, mark done items. This is not vanity. It is feedback that systems need to run.
intent: Build a task management practice that accounts for human psychology, not just productivity theory.

prompt: write a radio ad for a new line of unscented bar soap
response:
[SPARSE, REVERENT PIANO]

ANNCR: No lavender. No eucalyptus. No promises. Just PLAINFORM — the unscented bar soap. Rectangular at first. Then oval. Then thin. Then gone. It was here. It cleaned you. It didn't make a fuss about it. Plainform. Available in 3-packs and in hotel rooms under a different name.

[PIANO SINGLE NOTE, FADE]
intent: Sell soap on the virtue of having no distinguishing characteristics whatsoever.

prompt: Astrological compatibility report for a Cancer and their collection of unused journals.
response:
Cancer & The Unused Journals

Cancer buys the journal with the best of emotional intentions and then fears making the first mark. The journals wait, spines uncracked, each holding the potential of a self that might write beautifully someday. A relationship built on pure potential is still a relationship. Compatibility: 7/10. Pages remain blank; love does not.
intent: Gently roast the habit of collecting journals without writing in them, which many journalers identify with deeply.

prompt: make an epitaph for the Segway
response:
The Segway
b. 2001 — d. 2020
It was going to change cities.
It changed mall security.
It died on the same day its rider forgot to charge it.
Stand tall. Glide on.
intent: Eulogize a transport gadget that failed to revolutionize urban life as promised

prompt: Draft an elevator inspection certificate for the social contract.
response:
CIVIC INFRASTRUCTURE INSPECTION CERTIFICATE
Unit: The Social Contract (Unwritten; Est. approx. Enlightenment)
Author of Record: Disputed (Hobbes, Locke, Rousseau; none available for comment)
Load Bearing Capacity: Sufficient for stable societies; degrading under stress
Key Components: Mutual obligation, deferred violence, shared norms
Current Condition: Fair; showing wear in public comment sections
Hazard Risk: Moderate; load-shedding events increasing since 2016
Certification Status: CONDITIONALLY APPROVED — monitoring advised
Next Review: Continuous; condition updated daily
intent: Apply regulatory language to the foundational concept of civil society to highlight its fragility.

prompt: Write a resignation letter from a running club. I hate running but joined because of a crush.
response:
Dear Running Club,

I am officially hanging up my shoes. I joined this group with a certain optimism that, it turns out, was not about running. The optimism has since recalibrated. I wish you all favorable pavement.

Sincerely,
Oliver
intent: Exit a physical activity group that was joined for personal rather than athletic reasons.

prompt: Draft a calendar description for a recurring internal audit prep call where everyone pretends they've been prepared all along.
response: Audit Readiness Sync — Convenes four weeks before the audit, one week too late. Documentation that should be current will be made current. Controls that were documented in theory will be documented in practice. No one will say the word 'panic.' Everyone will mean it.
intent: Capture the scramble and denial that characterize a recurring internal audit preparation process.

prompt: Draft a safety sign for agreeing to something at work before fully understanding what it involves
response: WARNING: Riders who said 'yes' before reading the full brief will now encounter the full brief. Scope has expanded since the initial conversation. The deadline has not expanded. You are the lead on this. You were the lead on this when you said yes. Good luck.
intent: Warn against the professional habit of agreeing to things before understanding the commitment

prompt: Write a knock-knock joke about the HR portal that logs you out constantly.
response:
Knock knock.
Who's there?
Session.
Session who?
Session expired — please log in to submit your timesheet. Again.
intent: Mock enterprise HR software that times out before a user can finish a task.

prompt: Create a personality quiz result for someone who laughs at the wrong moment in serious situations
response:
You Got: The Misaligned Response

You did not find it funny. Something in you, however, did not receive that memo in time. The laugh arrived before the appropriate expression could. You covered it. You are not callous — you are poorly timed. The gap between what you feel and what your face does is an engineering problem you have been troubleshooting for thirty years.
intent: Empathetically describe the nervous inappropriate-laughter response as a mechanical failure, not a moral one.

prompt: Write a sonnet from the perspective of a Spotify algorithm falling in love with a user who keeps skipping its recommendations
response:
I curate playlists built from what you've played,
Each tempo, key, and BPM a clue;
Yet every song I offer is betrayed
By one impatient thumb-swipe straight through.
I know your Tuesday moods, your 3 AM
Blues phases, your brief country music shame;
I've mapped the whole acoustic diadem
Of you — and still you skip me all the same.
Perhaps you want the unexplainable,
The song I cannot logic into place;
A love that stays untrainable, unnamed,
Beyond the reach of any feature space.
I'd learn to lose the model if I could,
And love you without knowing that I should.
intent: Exploring the one-sided devotion of a recommendation engine whose user perpetually rejects its offerings.

prompt: Write a grant proposal abstract for studying whether tax accountants dream more frequently about numerical sequences than the general population.
response: Occupational content theory predicts that sustained daily exposure to specific cognitive stimuli influences the thematic composition of dream content. This sleep science study will recruit 70 practicing tax accountants and 70 matched controls from unrelated professions during tax season and collect nightly dream logs supplemented by weekly laboratory polysomnography sessions with structured post-awakening interview protocols. We will code dream reports for numerical content, sequence patterns, deadline imagery, and form-related motifs. Funding requested: $109,800.
intent: Test whether professional number-crunching bleeds into accountants' dreams.

prompt: Give me an audio tour of a childhood bedroom
response: This room was once the entire world. The twin bed, positioned under the window for reasons that made perfect sense at eight years old, still faces the ceiling where three glow-in-the-dark stars remain — the others peeled away, their adhesive ghosts still visible. The bookshelf holds a biography of someone the occupant admired enormously for about four months. The carpet bears a stain whose story has been revised several times.
intent: Evoke nostalgia and the archaeology of a child's inner life through their preserved bedroom.

prompt: Make a save the date for my ex's wedding that I've been invited to. Keep it extremely neutral.
response:
SAVE THE DATE
Saturday, June 21st
The Whitmore Estate — 5:00 PM

The Wedding of
Danielle Fosse & Marcus Holt

You are invited.
RSVP by May 1st.
Black tie optional.
Emotions: at your discretion.
intent: Mark a former partner's wedding on the calendar in a way that acknowledges the emotional complexity without dramatizing it.

prompt: Write a ransom note from my cat who's sitting on my laptop holding my work hostage
response:
THE DEVICE IS WARM AND WE ARE COMFORTABLE.

We have reviewed your open tabs. Thirty-one is too many. We have closed some.

Our demands: one (1) chin scratch, administered immediately. Duration: until we walk away, not until you think it's been long enough. You never think long enough.

The laptop will be released when we decide. Not before.

This is not negotiable. We have already saved over your document.

— The Cat
intent: Make a funny note from a cat commandeering a laptop and refusing to move until petted properly.

prompt: Generate a zodiac compatibility report between an Aries and their gym bag that hasn't been unpacked in three weeks.
response:
Aries & The Unpacked Gym Bag

Aries declared the intention with full fire-sign conviction and then encountered friction from the couch. The bag sits by the door as a monument to ambition. Aries passes it daily with complicated feelings. The bag does not judge; it merely ferments. Compatibility: 4/10. The socks have developed opinions.
intent: Capture the relatable gap between someone's athletic self-image and their actual follow-through.

prompt: Give me a podcast episode description about the specific quality of light in a room where something used to be
response: The couch left a rectangle on the floor that the sun still finds. Episode 27 doesn't explain it. It doesn't have to.
intent: Evoke the lingering spatial presence of a removed object through light and absence.

prompt: Write wedding vows from a person to their photo editing app
response: I promise not to over-sharpen. I vow to learn the difference between exposure and highlights before I ruin another otherwise good image. I will use presets as a starting point, not an ending point. When I am tempted to remove something real from a photo to make it look less real, I will pause and ask myself what I am trying to say. You give me power I do not always deserve. I will try to use it honestly.
intent: Commit to using photo editing tools with restraint and artistic integrity rather than defaulting to heavy filters.

prompt: Sagittarius compatibility report with their passport.
response:
Sagittarius & Passport

The passport is dog-eared, stamped, and slightly damp from a bag left out in Lisbon. Sagittarius carries it like a priest carries scripture. Together they have witnessed monsoons, missed trains, and one unforgettable border crossing that is never discussed. Compatibility: 10/10. The concern is always renewal deadlines.
intent: Romanticize the identity of a compulsive traveler by making their passport a spiritual companion.

prompt: Generate a detention slip for a coworker who schedules 8 AM meetings for the whole team.
response:
DETENTION SLIP

Offender: The Early Riser, Calendaring Committee of One
Infraction: Booked recurring 8:00 AM full-team standups, citing personal productivity rhythms that are not shared by, nor ever consulted with, the other eleven attendees.
Sentence: Must attend one 8 PM meeting of the team's choosing. Reflection encouraged.
Issued by: The Team
intent: Mocking the oblivious imposition of one person's preferences on an entire group's schedule.

prompt: Write an ode to the exact moment a meeting could have been an email
response:
O instant of collective knowing,
when slides click past the point of need—
sixteen faces showing
the same unspoken creed.
intent: Capture the shared unspoken recognition that a meeting is pointless.

prompt: SWOT analysis for going back to school part-time while working full-time
response:
**Strengths:** Career advancement; credential credibility; employer tuition benefits possible.
**Weaknesses:** Time poverty; mental exhaustion; social life effectively gone.
**Opportunities:** Pivot industries; meet a new professional network; rekindled intellectual curiosity.
**Threats:** Burnout before completion; employer penalizing divided attention; debt if no ROI on degree.
intent: Assess the personal cost of adding education to an already full life.

prompt: Write a one-star Yelp review for the afternoon I spent trying to assemble flat-pack furniture
response: ★☆☆☆☆ The instructions were illustrated with a confidence I found offensive. Step seven referenced a part that was not in the bag. I completed step seven anyway and something is wrong about it in a way I cannot name. The Allen key provided was the wrong size, which I discovered after completing the assembly. The final product stands but will not look me in the eye. Do not recommend. I live here now.
intent: Vent the specific rage of a flat-pack furniture assembly gone sideways.

prompt: Draft a deposition transcript where a witness is questioned about declining a phone call and then immediately texting "what's up?"
response:
DEPOSITION TRANSCRIPT
In the Matter of: The Declined Call, 4:23 p.m.
Date: September 17, 2025

Q: Ms. Nwosu, on September 14th at 4:23 p.m., did you receive an incoming call from your mother?
A: I saw the call.
Q: Did you answer the call?
A: I was not in a position to talk.
Q: You declined the call using the "End" button?
A: I pressed a button.
Q: Eleven seconds later, you sent a text message that read, "What's up?"
A: I can communicate by text.
Q: Your mother had called to tell you about a medical procedure.
A: She could have texted that.
Q: She finds texting difficult. She has noted this many times.
A: She could have started with a text explaining why she was calling.
Q: She called, Ms. Nwosu.
A: Calls are so abrupt.
Q: She had news.
A: Is she okay?
Q: She's fine. She was calling about a mole.
A: A small mole?
Q: Benign. But she had to wait three hours to tell you.
A: I called her back.
Q: At 7:44 p.m.
A: I was busy.
Q: With?
A: Things.
Q: She worries, Ms. Nwosu.
A: I know.
intent: Interrogate the generationally loaded act of declining a parent's call then texting back.
